metadata.title: Carrom Foul Checker Decision Tree
metadata.description: Decision tree for identifying fouls and their consequences based on the Laws of Carrom

scenarios.question: Did you pocket only the Striker (no C/m)?
scenarios.consequence: TURN LOST + One C/m as Due (penalty)
scenarios.details: Your turn ends. Opponent takes out one of your C/m as a Due penalty. If no C/m of yours is pocketed yet, the Due remains outstanding until one is available.

scenarios.question: Did you pocket the Striker WITH your own C/m?
scenarios.consequence: TURN CONTINUES + Pocketed C/m + Due taken out
scenarios.details: Your pocketed C/m goes back, plus one additional Due C/m. You continue playing.

scenarios.question: Did you pocket the Striker WITH opponent's C/m?
scenarios.consequence: TURN LOST + Opponent's C/m stays pocketed + Due
scenarios.details: Opponent's C/m is considered properly pocketed (good for them). You give up a Due C/m. Turn lost.

scenarios.question: Did you pocket the Striker with BOTH your own and opponent's C/m?
scenarios.consequence: TURN CONTINUES + Own C/m back + Due (opponent's stays)
scenarios.details: Your own C/m comes back along with Due C/m. Opponent's pocketed C/m stays pocketed. You continue playing.

scenarios.question: Did your Striker jump over the board?
scenarios.details: If you pocketed your own C/m or Queen in that stroke, your turn continues. Otherwise normal rules apply.

scenarios.question: Pocketed your own C/m by an improper stroke?
scenarios.consequence: TURN LOST + C/m + Due taken out
scenarios.details: C/m comes back plus a Due penalty. Turn ends.

scenarios.question: Pocketed opponent's C/m by an improper stroke?
scenarios.consequence: TURN LOST + C/m stays pocketed (for opponent) + Due
scenarios.details: Opponent's C/m is considered pocketed. You lose a Due C/m. Turn lost.

scenarios.question: Pushed the Striker instead of striking it?
scenarios.details: A 'push' (jerk or sudden elbow motion instead of finger flick) is a foul.

scenarios.question: Used your thumb to strike?
scenarios.details: Thumbing is defined in the Laws but not prohibited in standard play. Note: some tournaments restrict it. Stroke must still be made with finger movement.

scenarios.question: Striker not touching both Base Lines when struck?
scenarios.details: While taking a stroke, the Striker must touch both Base Lines.

scenarios.question: Striking from Base Circle but not covering it fully?
scenarios.details: If stroke is from Base Circle, Striker must cover Base Circle fully but not touch the arrow.

scenarios.question: Did your body (other than playing arm) touch the board/stand/table?
scenarios.details: During the board, only your playing arm may touch the board. Clothing/jewelry exempt but should not touch playing surface.

scenarios.question: Did body part (other than hand) cross imaginary arrow lines?
scenarios.details: Only the hand may go beyond imaginary lines of arrows.

scenarios.question: Did your elbow come within the playing surface or extend beyond arrow lines?
scenarios.details: Elbow of playing hand must not come within playing surface or extend beyond imaginary arrow lines. Hand may cross arrow.

scenarios.question: Took support from chair, stand, or table while striking?
scenarios.details: Cannot take support of stool/chair/stand/table or keep legs on rim. Hands may rest on body, legs may rest on rim of own seat.

scenarios.question: Got up from seat during own or opponent's turn?
scenarios.details: Lose board by number of your C/m + Queen on board. If opponent score 22+, lose by C/m only.

scenarios.question: Pocketed Queen before any of your C/m?
scenarios.consequence: TURN LOST + Queen taken out for placing
scenarios.details: Queen requires you to have at least one C/m pocketed first.

scenarios.question: Pocketed Queen while a Due was outstanding against you?
scenarios.details: Cannot pocket Queen with active Due penalty.

scenarios.question: Pocketed Queen but didn't cover it next stroke?
scenarios.details: Queen must be covered (your own C/m pocketed in same or immediately next stroke). If unnoticed before next stroke, recorded as covered.

scenarios.question: Pocketed Queen with Striker at break (all 9 C/m on board)?
scenarios.consequence: TURN LOST + Queen taken out + Due declared
scenarios.details: Special break rule for Queen + Striker combination.

scenarios.question: Used a Due/penalty C/m to set up a Queen shot?
scenarios.details: Cannot utilize Due/penalty C/m to make a shot with the Queen.

scenarios.question: Placed Due/penalty C/m outside the Outer Circle?
scenarios.details: Asked to place inside Outer Circle. Foul declared.

scenarios.question: Disturbed other C/m while placing?
scenarios.details: Original positions restored as far as possible. Foul declared.

scenarios.question: Placed Due/penalty C/m covering the Centre Circle?
scenarios.details: Cannot place wholly or partially on Centre Circle.

scenarios.question: Placed your own C/m by mistake (instead of opponent's)?
scenarios.details: If pointed out before next stroke, rectified + foul declared. If unnoticed before next stroke, placement valid.

scenarios.question: Hit, tapped, or disturbed the board?
scenarios.details: If done in way rearrangement impossible, lose entire board.

scenarios.question: Moved/touched C/m other than by stroke?
scenarios.details: C/m positions restored as far as possible. Foul declared.

scenarios.question: Tested the Striker's run on playing surface during the board?
scenarios.details: Run of Striker shall not be tested on playing surface during course of board.

scenarios.question: Removed powder by blowing/wiping during play?
scenarios.details: Cannot remove powder by blowing, wiping, or any other way. Excessive powder along your Base Lines/Circles can be removed with Striker only.

scenarios.question: Distracted opponent's attention?
scenarios.consequence: FOUL (escalates to match loss after warnings)
scenarios.details: More than twice despite Umpire's warning = lose entire match.

scenarios.question: Talked or gestured to partner in Doubles during board?
scenarios.details: Partners not allowed to talk or communicate by gestures during course of board.

scenarios.question: Held any solid material (other than Striker) during turn?
scenarios.details: Cannot hold solid material except Striker in either hand during turn.

scenarios.question: Kept Striker on frame or in pocket during board?
scenarios.details: Striker can only be placed on playing surface near own Base Line, while placing Due/penalty.

scenarios.question: Took more than 15 seconds for a stroke?
scenarios.details: Maximum 15 seconds from when previous C/m/Striker comes to rest and is picked up by opponent.

scenarios.question: Made the break before Umpire called 'Play'?
scenarios.details: All pocketed C/m taken out + additional C/m as penalty + Due declared.

scenarios.question: Took more than 15 seconds to place Due/penalty C/m?
scenarios.details: Time limit for placing Due/penalty is 15 seconds after Umpire's announcement.

scenarios.question: Did both players pass turns 3 consecutive times each?
scenarios.details: If players pass 3 times consecutively each, board cancelled and replayed.

scoring_scenarios.title: Complex Scoring Scenarios (Section XV)
scoring_scenarios.description: Specific Queen + final C/m combinations with point outcomes

scenarios.situation: Covering Queen + pocketing both your last C/m AND opponent's last C/m
scenarios.proper_stroke: Win by 3 points (or 1 point if score is 22+)
scenarios.improper_stroke: Opponent wins by 3 points (or 1 if 22+). Demanded: +1 additional point to opponent

scenarios.situation: Covering Queen + accidentally pocketing opponent's last C/m (not your own last)
scenarios.proper_stroke: Lose by your remaining C/m count + Queen value (or just C/m count if opponent at 22+)
scenarios.improper_stroke: Same as proper. Demanded: +1 point to opponent

scenarios.situation: Pocket Queen + your last C/m + opponent's last C/m together
scenarios.improper_stroke: Opponent wins by 3 (or loses by 1 if 22+). Demanded: +1 point

scenarios.situation: Pocket your last + opponent's last while Queen still on board
Pocket opponent's last C/m while Queen still on board (you have C/m left)
scenarios.proper_stroke: Lose by your remaining C/m + Queen value (just C/m if opponent 22+)

scenarios.situation: Pocket your last C/m leaving Queen on board
Pocket your last C/m WITH Striker leaving Queen on board
scenarios.proper_stroke: Lose by 3 points (1 if 22+). +1 demanded for Striker = 2 total
scenarios.improper_stroke: Lose by 3 (1 if 22+). +2 demanded = up to 5 points
